spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (window., SharedWorker) with runtime script (~6033 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (5 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (clients, difference, disconnect, extended, extendedlifetime, grace, immediately, keeps, period, resets, running, standar).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: Page does not include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link — violates the rule #3 self-heal invariant.

design_system.rationale: 3 raw hex colour(s) still in the style block. The design system requires CSS variables (`--bg-ivory`, `--text-charcoal` etc.)

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link
openQuestions.detail: Page is missing the chromestatus.com/feature/5138641357373440 link inside a `references` section. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires this on every concept so the slug-recovery script can re-derive the canonical name.

openQuestions.title: Swap raw hex colours for CSS variables
openQuestions.detail: Find the remaining hex literals in the inline `<style>` and replace each with the matching variable from `public/styles.css`. Re-check WCAG AA on any new pair you create.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("This update adds a new option, `extendedLifetime: true`, to the `SharedWorker` constructor. This new option requests that the shared worker be kept alive even after all current clients have unloaded. This allows pages to perform asynchronou") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/whatwg/html/commit/9c009049e4fa9dba638ef68ca502b781082bbb68 to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~6033 chars of script across 5 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 2 pass / 2 partial / 2 fail across the six rubric criteria.
